element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn six-button control scheme and basic attack patterns
Button combinations unlock character-specific special attacks; integrated into core combat loop
Health bar doubles as Ki meter; special move execution trades health for Ki recharge, creating resource management tension
Ki blast counterattacks triggered when both fighters strike simultaneously; rewards tactical timing
Ability to send opponent to back of stage adds positional depth absent from previous Butōden entries
Available only at critical health; high-risk comeback mechanic that intensifies match climax
Complex button combinations for maximum damage sequences; mastery separates skilled players
Sequential opponent progression; cumulative fatigue management across multiple consecutive matches

typical_relief_triggers: Health bar recovery via Ki recharge
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent health bar low and closing
Player health at critical state (desperation move available)
Rush Battle System simultaneous strike situations
Tournament mode: advancing to next bracket opponent after exhausting match
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-paced intensity with high volatility in final seconds; desperation moves and low-health mechanics create late-match tension escalation; tournament mode adds cumulative fatigue pacing across consecutive bouts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Story mode introduces basic one-on-one combat against progressively stronger opponents from Frieza saga onward
difficulty_curve.mid_game: AI opponents leverage special moves and Rush Battle System mechanics; character knockback positioning becomes tactically important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Story mode concludes with Majin Buu saga opponents; desperation moves and meteor combos required for victory; tournament mode tests sustained performance across eight consecutive matches
notable_spikes: Transition from basic combat to special move execution requirement
Introduction of Rush Battle System simultaneously striking scenarios
Late-match desperation move availability shifting match momentum
Tournament mode: fatigue accumulation across consecutive opponents without healing

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player campaign follows Dragon Ball Z narrative from Frieza saga through Majin Buu saga conclusion; each opponent represents canonical story antagonist or key character; unlocks roster characters sequentially
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Story mode bracket: Frieza → Perfect Cell → Majin Vegeta → Kid Buu

notable_level_patterns.description: Tournament mode requires player to defeat eight consecutive opponents in bracket elimination; no healing between matches; cumulative fatigue affects performance in later rounds
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament mode: sustained gauntlet requiring resource management across multiple bouts

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of ten roster characters (Goku, Majin Vegeta, Gotenks, Gohan, Vegito, Piccolo, Majin Buu, Kid Buu, Frieza, Perfect Cell) has unique special move combinations and meteor combo paths; gameplay loops around mastering individual character button sequences
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any match; character selection determines available special move repertoire
